name: pyvis (Interactive Network Visualization)

performance.notes: HTML/JS rendering; browser-based; struggles above 1000 nodes

agent_readiness.notes: Interactive network visualization generating HTML+JS via vis.js. from pyvis.network import Network. net = Network(height='750px', width='100%', directed=True). net.add_node(1, label='Node 1', color='#ff0000'). net.add_edge(1, 2, weight=3). net.from_nx(nx_graph) — import from NetworkX. net.show('graph.html'). Physics simulation: Barnes-Hut, ForceAtlas2. Jupyter notebook support: net.show('graph.html', notebook=True). Hover tooltips. Node grouping by color. Used for knowledge graphs, social networks, dependency graphs. Limited to ~500-1000 nodes for smooth interaction.

gotchas: Performance degrades above 500-1000 nodes in browser
Generated HTML embeds vis.js — large files for complex graphs
No backend rendering — requires browser to display
best_when: Interactive network visualization for Jupyter notebooks
Knowledge graph exploration with hover/click interactions
Sharing network visualizations as standalone HTML files
avoid_when: Large graphs (>1000 nodes) — use Gephi or sigma.js
summary: pyvis generates interactive HTML network visualizations via vis.js. from pyvis.network import Network. net.add_node(), net.add_edge(), net.from_nx(nx_graph), net.show('graph.html'). Physics-based layout (Barnes-Hut, ForceAtlas2). Hover tooltips, directed arrows, node colors/sizes. Works in Jupyter notebooks. Best for interactive exploration of small-to-medium graphs (<500 nodes). Standalone HTML output shareable without Python.
